articles.title: This Tiny Spider Named After Pink Floyd Hunts Bugs 6 Times Its Size!
articles.summary: A new spider as small as a grain of rice lives in walls and eats pests like flies and mosquitoes. Scientists named it after the band Pink Floyd. It can catch ants six times bigger than itself!

articles.title: Smart Underwear Counts Your Farts — For Science!
articles.summary: Scientists made a tiny sensor that snaps onto underwear to count farts. In a study, people averaged 32 toots a day. Now they want to find out what's normal for everyone.

articles.title: AI Can Launch Cyberattacks for Just $1 — Here's How We Fight Back
articles.summary: AI can now find and attack computer bugs in minutes, costing less than a dollar. But the same AI can also help defenders spot flaws early. The real challenge is fixing old code that volunteers maintain.
